triples.object: Marine ecosystems are the largest of Earth 's aquatic ecosystems and exist in waters that have a high salt content. triples.subject: Coral bleaching in Oahu

triples.subject: Resilience of coral reefs
triples.object: The resilience of coral reefs is the biological ability of coral reefs to recover from natural and anthropogenic disturbances such as storms and bleaching episodes. triples.subject: Tutia Reef
triples.object: Tutia Reef ( Miamba ya Tutia , in Swahili ) is a system of protected coral reefs inside the Mafia Island Marine Park (MIMP) with the IUCN category II designation. triples.subject: Ocean acidification in the Great Barrier Reef
triples.object: Ocean acidification threatens the Great Barrier Reef by reducing the viability and strength of coral reefs . triples.subject: Coral reefs of Jamaica
triples.object: Jamaica , an island located within the Caribbean Sea , known for being a popular tourist destination because of its pristine white sand beaches, is now faced with the issue of mass coral depletion. triples.subject: Southeast Asian coral reefs
triples.object: Southeast Asian coral reefs have the highest levels of biodiversity for the world's marine ecosystems. triples.subject: Coral in non-tropical regions

triples.subject: Environmental issues with coral reefs
triples.object: Human activities have substantial impact on coral reefs , contributing to their worldwide decline. [ 2 ] Damaging activities encompass coral mining , pollution (both organic and non-organic), overfishing , blast fishing , as well as the excavation of canals and access points to islands and bays. triples.subject: Belize Barrier Reef
triples.object: The Belize Barrier Reef is a series of coral reefs straddling the coast of Belize , roughly 300 metres (980 ft) offshore in the north and 40 kilometres (25 mi) in the south within the country limits. The Belize Barrier Reef is a 300-kilometre (190 mi) long section of the 900-kilometre (560 mi) Mesoa

triples.subject: Coral reefs of Kiribati
triples.object: The Coral reefs of Kiribati consists of 32 atolls and one raised coral island , Banaba (Ocean Island), which is an isolated island between Nauru and the Gilbert Islands . triples.subject: Ocean acidification in the Arctic Ocean
triples.object: The Arctic Ocean covers an area of 14,056,000 square kilometers, and supports a diverse and important socioeconomic food web of organisms, despite its average water temperature being 32 degrees Fahrenheit. triples.subject: European Project on Ocean Acidification
triples.object: The European Project on Ocean Acidification ( EPOCA ) was Europe's first major research initiative and the first large-scale international research effort devoted to studying the impacts and consequences of ocean acidification . [ 1 ] [ 2 ] EPOCA was an EU FP7 Integrated Project active during four y

triples.subject: Estuarine acidification
triples.object: Estuarine acidification happens when the pH balance of water in coastal marine ecosystems, specifically those of estuaries , decreases. triples.subject: Justin B. Ries
triples.object: Justin Baker Ries is an American marine scientist, best known for his contributions to ocean acidification , carbon sequestration , and biomineralization research.

triples.subject: Free Ocean CO2 Enrichment
triples.object: Free Ocean CO 2 Enrichment ( FOCE ) is a technology facilitating studies of the consequences of ocean acidification for marine organisms and communities by enabling the precise control of CO 2 enrichment within in-situ, partially open, experimental enclosures. triples.object: Tessa Michelle Hill is an American marine geochemist and oceanographer . She is a Fellow of the California Academy of Sciences , and in 2016 was named a Leshner Public Engagement F

triples.subject: Joan Kleypas
triples.object: Joan Ann ("Joanie") Kleypas is a marine scientist known for her work on the impact of ocean acidification and climate change on coral reefs, and for advancing solutions to environmental problems caused by climate change.

triples.subject: Fredrik Jutfelt
triples.object: Fredrik Jutfelt (born 1975) is a Swedish scientist and animal physiologist . He is a professor at the Department of Biological & Environmental Sciences at the University of Gothenburg [ 1 ] in Sweden and former professor at the Department of Biology at the Norwegian University of Science and Technol

triples.subject: Jean-Pierre Gattuso
triples.object: Jean-Pierre Gattuso ( French pronunciation: [ÊÉÌ pjÉÊ gatÉ¥zo] ) (born 14 December 1958 in Antibes ) is a French ocean scientist conducting research globally, from the pole to the tropics and from nearshore to the open ocean. His research addresses the biology of reef-building corals, the bioge

triples.subject: François M. M. Morel
triples.object: FranÃ§ois M. M. Morel (born 11 October 1944) is a French-American biogeochemist. [ 1 ] He is known for his research on ocean acidification , mercury pollution , the only known cadmium metalloenzyme , and the interactions between trace metals and microorganisms .

triples.subject: Rhodolith
triples.object: Rhodoliths (from Greek for red rocks ) are colorful, unattached calcareous nodules, composed of crustose , benthic marine red algae that resemble coral . Rhodolith beds create biogenic habitat for diverse benthic communities. The rhodolithic growth habit has been attained by a number of unrelated co

triples.subject: The Ocean Foundation
triples.object: The Ocean Foundation is a 501(c)(3) community foundation , based in Washington, D.C. and established in 2002. [ 1 ] Its mission is "to support, strengthen, and promote those organizations dedicated to reversing the trend of destruction of ocean environments around the world." [ 2 ]

triples.subject: Marine chemistry
triples.object: Marine chemistry , also known as ocean chemistry or chemical oceanography , is the study of the chemical composition and processes of the worldâs oceans, including the interactions between seawater, the atmosphere, the seafloor, and marine organisms. [ 2 ] This field encompasses a wide range of to

triples.subject: Living Oceans Society
triples.object: Living Oceans Society is a Canadian environmental organization that has been a leader in the effort to protect Canada's oceans since 1998. It is based in Sointula , British Columbia , with a satellite office in Vancouver , British Columbia. Living Oceans Society's vision states that: "Canada's ocean

triples.subject: Marine biogenic calcification
triples.object: Marine biogenic calcification is the production of calcium carbonate by organisms in the global ocean.

triples.subject: Alkalinity
triples.object: Alkalinity (from Arabic : Ø§ÙÙÙÙÙØ© , romanized : al-qaly , lit. â ' ashes of the saltwort ' ) [ 1 ] is the capacity of water to resist acidification .
